sections.subtitle: Team Form Analysis
sections.text: SV Zulte Waregem are in poor form, losing 3 of their last 5 matches, with a leaky defense conceding 12 goals. Oud-Heverlee Leuven have shown mixed results, winning 2 and losing 3 of their last 5, but they have struggled defensively as well, conceding 12 goals. Both teams have been involved in high-scoring games recently.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Analysis
sections.text: Oud-Heverlee Leuven dominate the recent head-to-head record against SV Zulte Waregem, winning both of the last 2 encounters with convincing scores (5-0 and 4-2). Historically, matches between these teams often see both teams scoring and over 2.5 goals, with Leuven unbeaten in their last 8 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Both teams have identical goals scored (7) and conceded (12) this season, ranking low in defensive metrics. Zulte Waregem struggle with possession (43%, 14th) and big chances created (14th), while Leuven have shown slightly better attacking output in recent games. Corners and cards are moderate for both, averaging around 4.6 corners per game each.

sections.subtitle: Key Trends and Streaks
sections.text: Zulte Waregem have failed to keep a clean sheet in 8 games and often concede first, while Leuven have a strong record against them without a loss in 8 head-to-heads. Both teams scoring is a common trend in their matchups (9/10), and games often feature high goal tallies.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, SV Zulte Waregem leads Oud-Heverlee Leuven by a comfortable margin of 2-0 at Elindus Arena. The home side capitalized on their opportunities with clinical finishing, scoring twice through Thomas Claes and Yannick Cappelle. Meanwhile, the away team struggled to convert their possession into meaningful chances, failing to trouble the scoreboard despite having more of the ball.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: SV Zulte Waregem has been dominant in key areas during the first half. Their attacking play was sharp, with significant entries into the penalty area and effective shots on target. Defensively, they’ve been solid, winning a high percentage of duels and aerial challenges, which has stifled Leuven's attempts to build momentum. Key players like Yannick Cappelle and Thomas Claes have stood out with their goal-scoring contributions and overall influence on the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance
halftimeSections.text: Oud-Heverlee Leuven has had a frustrating first half despite holding a slight edge in ball possession. They’ve managed numerous entries into the final third and penalty area, but their finishing has been lackluster, with only a couple of shots on target and a hit on the woodwork. Defensively, they’ve struggled to win duels and aerial battles, which has allowed Zulte Waregem to exploit gaps. Discipline has also been an issue with a couple of yellow cards picked up.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Zulte Waregem started with intent, showing early attacking spells that put pressure on Leuven's defense. However, the away side responded with sustained periods of control, pushing forward with intent for large stretches of the half. Despite this, Zulte Waregem regained the upper hand with crucial attacking bursts around the times of their goals, decisively shifting the momentum in their favor. Towards the end of the half, both teams traded short spells of dominance, but neither could create a defining moment before the whistle.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, SV Zulte Waregem will likely aim to protect their lead while looking for counter-attacking opportunities to extend it. Their defensive solidity and ability to win duels could frustrate Leuven further. On the other hand, Oud-Heverlee Leuven will need to be more clinical in front of goal and improve their duel-winning ratios to stage a comeback. Expect the away side to push harder, potentially leaving spaces at the back that Zulte Waregem could exploit. The game could open up as Leuven chases the deficit, increasing the likelihood of more scoring chances for both teams.
